I am a Graduate in Business Analytics from Arizona State University's W.P. Carey School of Business learning the art of analyzing data and creating actionable insights. In addition to my master's degree, I have completed my bachelor's in Computer Science, all of which has equipped me with strong technical, analytical and business skills. Skilled in recording, interpreting and analyzing data with proficiency in programming, statistical analysis and machine learning supported by strong communication skills. ### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/chriskanan_continuallearning-ai-deeplearning-activity-7125770462927675393-bzYd
...
Activity: John Waldo liked this
...
Our paper on computationally efficient continual learning has been accepted by TMLR. SIESTA is the first continual learning method to meet the needs of  industry - being more efficient than retraining from scratch. It works for arbitrary distributions, rather than only incremental class learning, and has the same performance as an offline model. Compared to other methods, SIESTA requires 7x-60x fewer updates, 10x less memory, 2x-20x fewer parameters, and works for arbitrary distributions, rather than for only extreme edge cases. Unlike many continual learning methods, it scales to real-world datasets rather than only toy problems. SIESTA requires only 1.9 hours to learn full ImageNet-1K whereas other methods require many hours or even days on the same hardware.
...
Project Website: https://
...
kd.in/eVUEbTN5 #continuallearning #ai #deeplearning #greenai
...
Activity: John Waldo liked this
...
I'm excited to visit UC San Diego this Friday! As an alum, it's been over a decade since I've been back to San Diego. I will be giving a talk at 2 PM in CSE 2154 titled "Unleashing Continual Deep Learning: Bridging AI's Gaps with Cognitive Science" where I will be recapping my post-PhD career and discussing some of my recent work in continual learning. I look forward to reconnecting with my old mentors and teachers. Hope to see you there!
...
I'll be giving another presentation with the University of Rochester's President Sarah Mangelsdorf on Generative AI and UR's new strategic plan on Saturday at the Museum of Contemporary Art San Diego and then on Sunday, we will give the same talk in Los Angeles at the Skirball Center. Looking forward to meeting UR alumni and trustees.

results.title: Emma Mitchell
highlights:
Computer Science and Machine Learning | Product Management @ CARVANA | AI, Consulting, Business Strategy
...
Associate Product Manager at [Carvana](https://www.linkedin.com/company/carvana)
...
I’m a computer scientist and engineer focused on building AI-powered systems and scalable software products that solve real-world problems. My work sits at the intersection of engineering, business strategy, and product development, where I enjoy translating complex challenges into practical, production-ready solutions. I recently graduated with a B.S. in Computer Science from the University of Florida, where I concentrated on artificial intelligence, product development, and data analytics. During my time there, I focused on building intelligent systems and software that can operate in real-world environments and support decision-making at scale. At EY, I work with Fortune 500 clients to analyze R&D activity, identify qualifying expenditures, and build the technical case for meaningful tax deductions — collaborating across engineering, finance, and legal teams to drive project deliverables from scoping through execution. I have also worked in an early-stage startup environment where I built engineering solutions that supported both product development and business growth. Working in a startup strengthened my ability to operate in fast-moving and ambiguous environments, build systems from the ground up, and communicate technical ideas clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Outside of my professional work, I enjoy building AI-driven applications and full-stack software systems. One project I developed is GatorNet AI, an LLM-powered campus assistant designed to help students navigate university resources and academic workflows. The system uses retrieval-augmented generation, vector embeddings, and semantic search to deliver contextual responses to student questions and has processed thousands of queries with high accuracy. I have also built a full-stack algorithmic trading platform that processes financial data across thousands of securities and visualizes market patterns through interactive dashboards. Through these experiences, I’ve developed strong interests in AI-powered product development, LLM applications, scalable backend systems, and building zero-to-one technology products.

### [Carvana](https://www.linkedin.com/company/carvana)

#### Software Engineering Team Lead (Current)

I lead a small team called the ROAD crew that specializes in prototyping, domain consulting, troubleshooting, and working with product managers on new lines of work for our platform team. While we are a technical team that does engineering work, our work tends to be short, high leverage, and somewhat unusual.
...
#### Senior Software Engineer
...
Have you ever bought a car? How hard was it to compare features and dollar amounts between makes and dealerships? Breaking through that nonsense is my job. My team manages over 30 microservices that source, massage, and distribute accurate and comparable data on millions of vehicles to the company and our customers so that we don't get those dealership blues.
...
- I am currently rearchitecting a core group of services to enable automatic statistical assessment of data quality, enhanced data access via graphql and elasticsearch, and mapping siloed oem language to a common vehicle data language.
...
- I won an award for helping other teams integrate with our data and resolve issues.
...
- I improved the performance (15-5s -> 1s-100ms), uptime (~70% -> 99.9%), accuracy (~75% -> 99+%), scalability (10k -> 6mil reqs/day) of a key python microservice that performs fuzzy text matching by edit distance from raw data to our internal vehicle language.
...
- I stood up one of the first graphql endpoints at the company on a kubernetes cluster to distribute our data, and the first service on our team on the k8s cluster
...
- I used k6 load testing with our graphql endpoint to identify performance bottlenecks in our services, and laid the groundwork for other services to use load testing.
...
- I created a custom logging implementation for python that supports structured, contextual logging in multithreaded and multiprocessing contexts and used it in our services.
...
- I designed an ETL framework in python that rips data from our services at configurable scale and stores it in a SQL Server database for analysts bound to SQL queries.
...
- I redid the rules engine for computing damages on a vehicle to be extensible, and maintainable.
...
- I refactored the web scraper for our biggest auction source from taking 10hrs to 1hr to reprocess 1/4 mil listings a day
...
- I migrated our team from MySQL to SQL Server and implemented the tools and processes for running migrations on the database. Show less

results.title: Data Science (Generative AI) - Capital One Auto Finance | Wfh
highlights:
# Data Science (Generative AI) - Capital One Auto Finance
...
Data is at the center of everything we do. As a startup, we disrupted the credit card industry by individually personalizing every credit card offer using statistical modeling and the relational database, cutting edge technology in 1988! Fast-forward a few years, and this little innovation and our passion for data has skyrocketed us to a Fortune 200 company and a leader in the world of data-driven decision-making. As a Data Scientist at Capital One, you’ll be part of a team that’s leading the next wave of disruption at a whole new scale, using the latest in computing and machine learning technologies and operating across billions of customer records to unlock the big opportunities that help everyday people save money, time and agony in their financial lives. As a Data Scientist at Capital One's Auto Finance business, you’ll be part of a high performing modeling and analytics team that’s leading the next wave of disruption at a whole new scale. Our team has a relentless focus on the craft of modeling and innovation, using the latest in computing and machine learning technologies and operating across billions of customer records to unlock the big opportunities that help everyday people save money, time and agony in their financial lives. You’ll drive the heart of business by working on an array of impactful and exciting applications like customer lifetime valuation, product recommendation, fraud detection, and productivity improvement via Generative AI. In this role, you will: Partner with a cross-functional team of data scientists, software engineers, machine learning engineers and product managers to deliver AI powered products that change how customers interact with their money. Leverage a broad stack of open source and cloud technologies to reveal the insights hidden within huge volumes of numeric and textual data. Drive the development of customer-facing Generative AI applications, including novel agentic AI solutions, by expertly finetuning Large Language Models and applying reinforcement learning. Build AI/ML solutions through all phases of development, from design through training, evaluation, and validation; partnering with engineering teams to operationalize them in scalable and resilient production systems that serve 80+ million customers. Flex your interpersonal skills to translate the complexity of your work into tangible business goals. The Ideal Candidate is: Customer first. You love the process of analyzing and creating, but also share our passion to do the right thing. You know at the end of the day it’s about making the right decision for our customers. Innovative. You continually research and evaluate emerging technologies. You stay current on published state-of-the-art methods, technologies, and applications and seek out opportunities to apply them. Creative. You thrive on bringing definition to big, undefined problems. You love asking questions and pushing hard to find answers. You’re not afraid to share a new idea. Technical. You’re comfortable with advanced ML and DL technologies including language models and are passionate about developing further. You have hands-on experience working with LLMs and solutions using open-source tools and cloud computing platforms. Influential. You are passionate about AI/ML and can bring along a cross functional team in breakthrough innovations. You communicate clearly and effectively to share your findings with non-technical audiences. You are experienced in training and fine tuning large language models or large multimodal models as well as have expertise in one or more key subdomains such as: Reinforcement Learning, Responsible AI, Computer Vision, RLHF. You have an engineering mindset as shown by a track record of delivering models at scale both in training data and inference volumes. You have experience in delivering libraries, platforms, or solution level code to existing products.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
highlights:
Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
...
# Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization
...
Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence are at the heart of the Airbnb product. From Trust to Payments, and from Customer Service to Marketing we rely on ML to ensure that guests and hosts have the best possible experience with Airbnb.
...
The CS AI product team is responsible for driving CSxAI (Customer Support x Artificial Intelligence) initiatives by adopting the Generative AI technologies to enable an intelligent, scalable and exceptional service experience. The team develops and enhances various AI models, ML services and tools including LLM fine-tuning, alignment and optimization, RAG/Search, LLM evaluation and testing automation, feedback-based learning and guardrail for a wide range of applications in Airbnb.
...
As a principal machine learning engineer, you will be responsible for fine-tuning state-of-the-art LLMs for diverse use cases while optimizing models for high-performance deployment on Airbnb’s ML Infrastructure. You will partner with product managers, software engineers, data scientists and operation teams to brainstorm, design and develop AI products such as AI Assistant, Autonomous agent, recommendation, travel planning, and many more products that make meaningful impacts in the world of travel.
...
- Work with large scale structured and unstructured data; explore, experiment, build and continuously improve foundation models for Airbnb product, business and operational use cases.
...
- Create a multi-year tech roadmap that enables our team to stay on the leading edge of the rapidly evolving AI landscape and leverage the best in class technologies to deliver customer benefits.
...
- Continuously evaluate recent and upcoming large foundational models, ensuring the selection and refinement of the highest quality models for enhanced performance and efficiency.
...
- Hands-on prototype, develop and productionize LLM models and pipelines at scale, including both batch and real-time use cases.
...
- Drive key AI architectural decisions for products, and contribute to Airbnb’s ML platform architecture and strategy.
...
- Minimum Qualifications:
...
- PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Statistics, or related technical field.
...
- 10+ years of experience with developing machine learning models and products at scale from inception to business impact.
...
- Programming experience in Python and hands-on experience with frameworks such as PyTorch.
...
- Proven record of training, fine tuning, optimizing models and inference run-time
...
- Post-training experience in areas like data processing for fine-tuning; responsible LLMs; LLM alignment; reinforcement learning; efficient training and inference; language model evaluation; and/or multilingual and multimodal modeling.
...
- Or specialized experience in runtime optimizations, model quantization, compression, on-device inference, GPU inference, pytorch, kernel development
...
- Preferred Qualifications:
...
- PhD in AI, machine learning, data science, or related technical fields.

- Publications at peer-reviewed AI conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, CVPR, ICML, ICLR, ICCV, and ACL).
...
- Customer Support Systems: Experience with AI technologies in customer support applications.
...
- Agile Practice for AI production: Experience with the entire AI product development lifecycle from incubation to production at scale, following agile practices in the Applied AI/ML domain.
...
- Infrastructure Acumen: Experience deploying and scaling business-critical AI services and driving architectural requirements on ML infrastructures
